era: 1993-2002. Mathcore / Post-Hardcore. E=9 -- maximum energy. Four-degree E=9 arc closes here. We Are the Romans in full canonical context. Tacoma.
associated_musicians: These Arms Are Snakes (Brian Cook post-Botch)

theory_nodes.concept: E=9 arc closes at degree 289. Four-degree streak: Dead Kennedys (286) → Black Flag (287) → Blood Brothers (288) → Botch (289). Botch is the terminus: hardcore's aggression + compositional intelligence = mathcore peak.
theory_nodes.description: The E=9 streak ends at maximum energy with Tacoma mathcore.

theory_nodes.concept: Brian Cook bridge: Botch bassist founds These Arms Are Snakes immediately after dissolution in 2002. Engine's most direct personnel bridge: same musician, consecutive degrees 289→290.
theory_nodes.description: The direct personnel bridge to degree 290.

theory_nodes.concept: We Are the Romans canonical growth: released 1999, grew in stature every year, confirmed canonical by 2022 reunion shows. Two generations of heavy bands cite it as primary influence.
theory_nodes.description: Record ahead of its moment, confirmed over 25 years.

theory_nodes.concept: Dissolution as artistic integrity: Botch chose to dissolve in 2002 rather than continue past creative peak. No new studio material at either reunion. Catalog complete and finished.
theory_nodes.description: Ending at the right time preserves the catalog.

cross_references.relationship: E=8 at first appearance. Mathcore established. We Are the Romans introduced. Tacoma enters engine. Return at 289 raises to E=9.
E=9 streak continues from 286. Chaotic post-hardcore. 289 receives the streak and closes it.
Brian Cook direct bridge: Botch bassist founds TAAS 2002. Most direct personnel bridge in engine. E=8. Capitol Hill. Suicide Squeeze.

sps_pair.pairing_note: FIRST LARIDAE. E=6. E=9+E=6=E=15 combined. Botch's mathematical precision vs. organic colonial cacophony of hundreds of overlapping gull calls. Maximum musical energy meets maximum colony sound volume.
